meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Level Up Your Coding Workflow
content.seo_description: Break through your coding plateau. Learn to debug complex apps using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate guide for aspiring AI software engineers.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracing logs. Use AI-driven observability to map your execution flow, identify silent failures, and generate precise patches. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for iterative repairs, you transform debugging from a frustrating bottleneck into a rapid, logic-driven development cycle that accelerates your project delivery.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours staring at a terminal flickering with cryptic stack traces, feeling the familiar weight of a project that has stalled. You have the syntax down, but you’re stuck in the 'tutorial purgatory'—the gap between building a simple to-do list and architecting a real-world SaaS application. It’s not your code that’s broken; it’s your feedback loop.

The secret to moving from student to engineer isn't knowing every library—it's mastering the art of the diagnostic. By leveraging the latest AI-native development tools, you can externalize your cognitive load, letting machines handle the grunt work of tracing state changes while you focus on the architecture that actually scales.

steps.title: Contextualize the Failure
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the scope. Map the failure to a specific function or module to prevent AI hallucination.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and create a dependency graph for the authentication flow. Identify where state is lost during the redirection process.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Map the auth module state flow and identify the entry point of the current bug.'

steps.title: Automated Log Injection
steps.description: Inject structured logging at high-probability failure points to capture runtime context that isn't immediately visible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest 3 strategic locations for structured logging in the current data pipeline to capture runtime object state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --instruction 'Insert structured logs at the identified points in the data pipeline.'

steps.title: Isolation Testing
steps.description: Create a minimal reproduction case to isolate the bug from the noise of the rest of the application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a unit test suite that mocks the API response to isolate the UI rendering logic from the backend dependencies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create a Jest test file that reproduces the rendering bug in isolation.'

steps.title: Iterative Patching
steps.description: Apply a targeted fix using Claude Code, ensuring that the fix includes regression tests to verify no secondary systems break.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this proposed patch for potential side effects on the user session storage. Suggest an alternative if necessary.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --instruction 'Apply the fix for the rendering bug and add a regression test.'

steps.title: Architectural Hardening
steps.description: Refactor the surrounding code to prevent similar bugs, turning a fix into a permanent architectural improvement.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current module to use a more robust error handling pattern to prevent silent failures in the future.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Refactor the auth module to implement robust error handling and type safety.'

pro_tips: Always treat your AI agent as a junior developer: provide the error logs, the expected output, and the last three successful commits.
Use 'Chain of Thought' prompting in Antigravity to force the AI to explain its reasoning before proposing a code change.
Never accept a patch without asking for a 'diff' explanation to ensure you understand the logic shift.
Keep your context window clean by clearing old, irrelevant logs before starting a new debugging session.
Use AI to document your fixes automatically—this builds your portfolio while solving your current bottleneck.
content.next_steps: Once your bug is squashed, document the resolution in your project README and commit your changes with a detailed technical note. Use this win to start a new feature branch and apply these same AI-assisted debugging patterns to your next challenge.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the technique, and the roadmap to break through the intermediate barrier and start shipping production-grade software. Stop struggling with errors and start building the future. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Inject structured logging at high-probability failure points to capture runtime context that isn't immediately visible.
Create a minimal reproduction case to isolate the bug from the noise of the rest of the application.
Apply a targeted fix using Claude Code, ensuring that the fix includes regression tests to verify no secondary systems break.
Refactor the surrounding code to prevent similar bugs, turning a fix into a permanent architectural improvement.
